headline: India Expands Strategic Oil Reserves With New ONGC Storage Plan
summary_raw: India’s ONGC plans to build up a strategic national reserve of crude totalling 1.75 million tons, or 12.8 million barrels. The company would also ask the government to allow the commercial use of the stockpile, Reuters reported. India currently has a national crude oil reserve of 5.33 million tons, which is equal to about 39 million barrels, in three locations in the southern part of the country.
